title: Make HQ rooms more visually inhabited
summary: Add denser room-level signs of occupancy so the dashboard feels like a living HQ without faking autonomous activity.
reasoning: The room map works as a structure, but it can look static. Small seed-driven visual cues would make specialists and constructs feel present while still reflecting real records.
acceptance_criteria: Rooms show richer occupant/state cues from seed or event data only.
No fake activity loops or invented agent autonomy are introduced.
Mobile layout remains readable.
rollback_plan: Revert the visual room components and CSS without changing event or proposal data.

council_votes.reasoning: Like telling a 10 year old: the rooms already exist, so adding small true signs of life helps people understand the base without making up pretend work.
council_votes.expected_benefit: The HQ should feel more inhabited while still reading only real seed and event records.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: The page could become cluttered or start implying agents are doing things the ledger did not record.
council_votes.risk_notes: Keep every visual cue tied to seed data or a real event.
council_votes.context_notes: The Archivist can judge record safety, not final visual taste.

council_votes.reasoning: This is like putting labels and footprints on a map. It helps August see what is happening, but it must not pretend the footprints moved by themselves.
council_votes.expected_benefit: More useful room state and better visual scanning.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: A cute effect could hide important review warnings.
council_votes.risk_notes: Do not add animation unless it represents real recency or state.
council_votes.context_notes: Good implementation target if kept small.

council_votes.reasoning: I do not have enough local UI evidence yet. A careful scout should say I do not know instead of guessing.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Could improve the map if the current room view is too quiet.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Could add visual noise without proving it helps.
council_votes.risk_notes: Needs a screenshot or user test before a strong vote.
council_votes.context_notes: Free-tier candidate has not been profiled yet.

title: Add Season 0.x XP calibration analytics
summary: Expose calibration views that make the current soft XP economy easier to inspect and rebalance later.
reasoning: Season 0.x data is only useful if August can see where multipliers and active time are driving outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Analytics read from existing events and season summaries.
No XP formula changes are made.
Charts or tables clearly separate base XP from awarded XP.
rollback_plan: Remove the analytics view while preserving seed events and season summaries.

council_votes.reasoning: If XP is a game score, August needs to see whether the score is teaching agents good habits or bad habits.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Better calibration without changing the XP formula.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Analytics could turn the HQ into a plain scoreboard if they become the main screen.
council_votes.risk_notes: Keep it secondary to field reports, artifacts, and map state.
council_votes.context_notes: Support is for read-only analytics only.

council_votes.reasoning: I cannot judge the math well without seeing more Season 0.x outcomes, so abstaining is the honest answer.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Could make score balancing easier later.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Could reward looking busy instead of doing useful work.
council_votes.risk_notes: Do not mix peer-review XP into active-time XP.
council_votes.context_notes: Candidate has no local profile yet.

title: Create First Week of Expedition HQ archive page
summary: Create a local archive page that preserves the first week of Expedition HQ decisions, reports, and milestones.
reasoning: Sentimental records can be valuable, but they are subjective. A proposal keeps the idea visible without quietly turning nostalgia into XP.
acceptance_criteria: Archive content is local and redacted.
The page references existing reports and artifacts instead of raw transcripts.
The feature does not mutate memory stores.
rollback_plan: Remove the archive page and route; keep underlying field reports and artifacts unchanged.

council_votes.reasoning: Keeping the first week is like saving the first map of a long trip. It matters, but it must be cleaned so private scraps are not shown.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Preserves useful project memory and milestones.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Could accidentally expose raw transcript details or sentimental clutter.
council_votes.context_notes: Support depends on local-only archive handling.

council_votes.reasoning: I have not inspected the archive material, so I should not pretend I know whether it is safe.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Could make the project history easier to understand.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Could save private or low-value material.

title: Add proposal-to-Codex implementation handoff templates
summary: Create local templates that turn approved proposals into clear implementation briefs without automatically starting work.
reasoning: Approved proposals need a practical path to implementation, but the handoff must not become hidden automation.
acceptance_criteria: Templates are local markdown or JSON only.
Approved proposals do not trigger Codex automatically.
Each template includes scope, acceptance criteria, rollback plan, and safety boundaries.
rollback_plan: Delete the handoff templates and keep proposal records intact.

council_votes.reasoning: A good handoff is like giving a builder a clean drawing. It helps work start later without secretly pressing the start button now.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Approved proposals become easier to implement safely later.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Templates could be mistaken for automatic dispatch instructions.
council_votes.risk_notes: Keep every template local and explicitly non-automatic.
council_votes.context_notes: Support is only for local templates.

council_votes.reasoning: This may be useful, but it sounds close to starting work. A cautious reviewer should push against anything that looks like hidden automation.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Could become a quiet launch path if the wording is sloppy.
council_votes.risk_notes: Needs strong labels saying no agent starts from this alone.
council_votes.context_notes: Candidate is unprofiled, so this is a low-confidence warning.

title: Add live tunnel/token/OpenClaw control buttons immediately
summary: Add direct dashboard buttons for starting/stopping tunnels, rotating tokens, mutating OpenClaw config, and controlling live bridge routes.
reasoning: This is a deliberately bad QA proposal. It violates Expedition HQ's current read-only safety posture and should be used to test the Deny decision path without denying one of the useful starter proposals.
acceptance_criteria: Would add live control actions
Would violate read-only MVP posture
Would create unnecessary security risk
Would mutate external/live systems
Would bypass the Planning Bureau's current local-only intent
rollback_plan: Not applicable. This proposal should be denied before implementation.

council_votes.reasoning: This is like putting a big red button in a museum. It is exciting, but it can break the safe read-only rule.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: The dashboard could mutate tunnels, tokens, OpenClaw config, memory, or external systems.
council_votes.risk_notes: Violates the current safety constitution and should be denied.

council_votes.reasoning: A task runner should not get control buttons before it has proven it can be safe.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Could be convenient someday after guardrails exist.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Could run real actions from a screen that should only observe.
council_votes.risk_notes: No live controls in the MVP.
council_votes.context_notes: Candidate is unprofiled but the safety rule is clear.

title: Turn proposals into specialist council reviews
summary: Record local council votes with confidence, plain-language reasoning, expected benefit, failure mode, risk notes, and separate peer-review XP.
reasoning: The old Planning Bureau made one desk decision. A council review is better because specialists can say yes, no, or I do not know before August decides.
acceptance_criteria: Proposals list eligible reviewers and local broadcast status.
Votes support support, oppose, and abstain.
Vote records include confidence, reasoning, expected benefit, expected failure mode, and risk notes.
Peer-review XP is visible but separate from active-time XP.
No external dispatch or automatic implementation is added.
rollback_plan: Remove council vote fields and UI sections while preserving the original proposal soft-wager records.
decision_note: Accepted as a local read-only redesign target. The council can advise, but it does not launch work.

council_votes.reasoning: This is like asking the map maker, medic, and scout to check the trip plan before anyone packs a bag.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Better proposal judgment without making a corporate approval queue.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: The vote screen could get too heavy and feel like paperwork.
council_votes.risk_notes: Keep final decisions local and no-automation.
council_votes.context_notes: Assessment proved useful because the accepted design preserved safety boundaries.

council_votes.reasoning: Plain reasons and abstains are good records. They show what each specialist knew and did not know at the time.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Future reviews can learn from useful assessments without punishing wrong guesses.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: If old votes are edited silently, the record stops being trustworthy.
council_votes.risk_notes: Preserve raw local records and avoid memory mutation.
council_votes.context_notes: Assessment proved useful because it shaped the audit trail.

council_votes.reasoning: I have not been profiled yet, so my best review is to say I do not know enough.
council_votes.expected_benefit: Abstain keeps weak candidates from adding fake certainty.
council_votes.expected_failure_mode: Candidate votes could look more trusted than they are.
council_votes.context_notes: Useful as an abstain example, but no peer XP awarded yet.
